name: Zalbizkar, Pagogan, and Kastañarri Loop
name_original: Zalbizkar, Pagogan y Kastañarri — circular por los Montes Altos de Vitoria
region: Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria
description: Hike a moderate 7.0-mile circular route in the Montes Altos de Vitoria, summiting Zalbizkar and Pagogan with forest and mountain views.
region_intro: Urban hiking trails in Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria offer a blend of city green spaces and accessible natural areas. The region is characterized by its extensive Green Belt, which includes wetlands, gall-oak forests, and riverine paths, providing varied environments for exploration. Beyond the urban perimeter, the Montes de Vitoria feature higher elevations and forests. This area provides diverse terrain for urban hikers, from flat paths to gentle ascents. Best urban hiking trails in Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria The most popular urban hiking route is Interior Basque Way of St. James: Vitoria-Gasteiz, an easy 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete, exploring urban areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Arquillos – Villa Suso Palace loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz, an easy 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path through the city's historical landmarks. Local hikers also love the Museum of Fine Arts of Álava – Basilica of Armentia loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through urban paths and green spaces, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking in Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria is defined by urban green belts, riverine paths, and accessible forests, with options for various ability levels. The routes in Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 480 reviews. More than 3900 hikers have used komoot to explore Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many urban hiking trails are available in Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria?
A: There are over 100 urban hiking trails in the region, offering a wide variety of experiences. You'll find routes ranging from easy strolls through city parks to more moderate paths that venture into the peri-urban green belt.

Q: Are there easy urban hikes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, the region offers many easy urban hikes perfect for families and beginners. Nearly half of the trails are rated as easy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055962567/interior-basque-way-of-st-james-vitoria-gasteiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Interior Basque Way of St. James: Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 5.4 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40356415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Arquillos – Villa Suso Palace loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant 5.3 km walk through historical areas.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on urban hikes here?
A: Urban hikes in Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria often blend cityscapes with natural beauty. You can explore the extensive Green Belt, which includes wetlands like Salburua, gall-oak forests, and riverine paths along the Zadorra River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31439990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Museum of Fine Arts of Álava – Basilica of Armentia loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you through a beautiful gall-oak forest.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the urban trails?
A: Absolutely. Many urban trails offer glimpses of historical sites and provide scenic viewpoints. You can climb Olarizu Hill for panoramic views of Vitoria-Gasteiz, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4194432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco Navarro Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40356415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Arquillos – Villa Suso Palace loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past significant historical architecture within the city.

Q: Are the urban hiking trails dog-friendly?
A: Many urban trails in the region are suitable for dogs, especially those within the Green Belt and peri-urban parks. It's always a good idea to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near roads, and to check local regulations for specific parks or nature reserves.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about urban hiking in this region?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 500 reviews. Reviewers often praise the accessibility of nature from the city, the well-maintained paths, and the diverse landscapes that seamlessly blend urban and natural environments.

Q: Are there circular urban walks available?
A: Yes, many urban routes are designed as circular walks, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40356415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Arquillos – Villa Suso Palace loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31439990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Museum of Fine Arts of Álava – Basilica of Armentia loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering convenient circular paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for urban hiking in Gasteizko Mendi Garaiak Kbe/Zec Montes Altos De Vitoria?
A: The region is enjoyable for urban hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming flowers or colorful foliage. Summer can be warm, making early mornings or late afternoons ideal, while milder winter days can also be suitable for a refreshing walk.

Q: Is public transport available to reach urban trailheads?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz has a good public transport network, including buses and trams, which can take you to various points within the city and its Green Belt, providing access to many urban trailheads. This makes it convenient to start your hike without needing a car.

Q: Are there cafes or amenities near the urban hiking trails?
A: Given the urban focus, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and other amenities within Vitoria-Gasteiz itself, often close to or along many of the urban hiking routes. The city center, which many trails pass through or start from, offers a wide selection of places to refresh and refuel.

Q: What are some longer urban hiking options for those seeking a moderate challenge?
A: For those looking for a longer urban hike, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23822338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendizorroza – Empty Avenue with Bus Lane loop&lt;/a&gt;, at nearly 12.5 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31452000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of Armentia – Mendizorroza loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 12.7 km, offer moderate challenges with extended distances through varied urban and peri-urban landscapes.
tips: Great views. It is a pity that there are two palomeras on the top.
Modest peak but with great views, and very close to the Pagogán
The top of Pagogan is easily accessible and offers great views, especially to the north and south.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Zalbizkar, Pagogan y Kastañarri circular hike?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate hike. It requires a good level of fitness, and while paths are mostly accessible, sure-footedness is needed in some sections. It's comparable to SAC 2-3 on the Swiss Alpine Club Hiking Scale.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain through the high mountains of Vitoria. You'll walk through diverse forest sections, including beech ('hayedo') and kermes-oak forests. Some parts feature visible limestone ('caliza') and may follow ancient medieval paths. Expect mostly accessible paths, but some sections require careful footing.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The circular route takes you to several prominent summits, including Zalbizkar (1,046 m), Pagogan, and Kastañarri. You'll also enjoy views of the Montes de Vitoria, diverse forest landscapes, and potentially picturesque waterfalls. Parts of the trail have historical significance, possibly following ancient medieval roads used for transporting limestone.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Montes Altos de Vitoria offer beautiful scenery throughout the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage. During winter, be prepared for snow conditions, especially at higher elevations. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations are not detailed, the trail is popular among local hikers in the Vitoria-Gasteiz area, suggesting accessible starting points. It's recommended to check local maps or komoot's route planner for designated parking areas near the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and respect other hikers. Be mindful of any specific local regulations regarding dogs in the Montes de Vitoria natural area.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for hiking in the Montes de Vitoria?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this route in the Montes de Vitoria. The area is generally open for public access. However, always respect local regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this circular route overlap with other established trails. Approximately 16% of the route follows the Artzaintza naturbidea, 15% is part of the GR282, and 2% coincides with the Aberasturi-Izartza trail.

faqs.question: Are there any historical aspects to this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of the trail may follow ancient medieval roads, such as 'El Camino de la Piedra' (The Stone Path). This route was historically used in the 13th and 14th centuries to transport limestone from the quarries of Ajarte for the construction of the Cathedral of Santa María in Vitoria.

faqs.question: Is there any public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail is located in the Montes Altos de Vitoria, which is accessible from Vitoria-Gasteiz, direct public transport to the exact trailhead might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to nearby villages or consider driving to the starting point.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this moderate hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing appropriate hiking footwear with good grip, layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather, sufficient water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot), and sun protection. Trekking poles can also be helpful on varied terrain.
